name: Institute for Understanding Anti-Palestinian Racism
theoryOfChange: The Institute for the Understanding of Anti-Palestinian Racism (IUAPR) utilizes a defined framework for understanding and addressing anti-Palestinian racism (APR). Their work, including research, education, and advocacy, is based on the description of anti-Palestinian racism as 'a form of racism that “silences, excludes, erases, stereotypes, defames or dehumanizes Palestinians or their narratives”'. This definition, which they explicitly state their surveys are designed in consultation with and based on, serves as their foundational strategic framework. The IUAPR's mission is to end racism against Palestinians and their allies, and they are committed to empirically researching, educating, and advocating on the impact of anti-Palestinian racism across all sectors of society. Their website features a 'Research & Resources' section with various reports and guides that demonstrate their structured approach to understanding and countering anti-Palestinian racism.
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2024 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.

evaluationSources: UNCORROBORATED: UCSF Institutional Review Board (IRB) approval for research

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects an emerging organization with <cite id="10">unverified outcomes</cite> that are currently documented but not yet externally evaluated.

impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that research-based advocacy actually results in policy changes at the institutional level.

strengths.detail: The charity fills a niche gap by focusing on <cite id="1">empirical research and data collection</cite> specifically related to anti-Palestinian racism.
The organization maintains a <cite id="5">documented theory of change</cite> that guides its advocacy and educational training programs.

strengths_deep_dive: The organization's primary strength lies in its ability to produce high-level academic and policy-oriented content. Its research has directly influenced academic governance, with <cite id="3">40 faculty associations utilizing its frameworks</cite> to navigate complex definitions of racism. By focusing on <cite id="2">naming, framing, and manifestations</cite> of bias, the Institute provides a scholarly foundation for broader advocacy efforts.
rich_narrative.summary: The Institute for the Understanding of Anti-Palestinian Racism Inc is a specialized research and advocacy organization focused on <cite id="1">addressing systemic bias through empirical research</cite>. Operating as an emerging institution, the charity focuses on <cite id="2">documenting and countering anti-Palestinian sentiment</cite> through three primary pillars: empirical research, education and training, and strategic advocacy. A significant portion of its early impact is evidenced by its work with academic institutions, where it reported that <cite id="3">40 faculty associations have rejected the IHRA definition</cite> of antisemitism in favor of frameworks that protect Palestinian speech. The organization operates as a <cite id="4">capacity-building entity</cite>, providing the data and framing necessary for other civil rights groups to challenge discriminatory practices. While the organization is in a growth phase, it maintains a <cite id="5">clear theory of change</cite> centered on the belief that naming and framing racism is the first step toward policy and social change. Currently, the organization <cite id="6">accepts sadaqah and general charitable contributions</cite> to fund its ongoing research initiatives and the development of educational materials designed for public and academic use.
